solute.description: This molecule is a flavone consisting of a benzopyrone core (chromone) substituted with a phenyl ring at the C2 position. It features a ketone at the C4 position and two vicinal hydroxyl groups forming a catechol moiety at the 6 and 7 positions of the fused benzene ring. The structure is a planar, medium-sized aromatic system characterized by high polarity and significant hydrogen-bonding capacity through its three oxygen-containing functional groups. Its distinctive character arises from the extended pi-conjugation across the entire tricyclic framework and the specific ortho-arrangement of the phenolic groups.

solvent.description: The molecule is a linear, unbranched chain consisting of six carbon atoms connected by single bonds. It is a saturated hydrocarbon (alkane) devoid of functional groups, branching, or cyclic structures. Due to the exclusive presence of C-C and C-H bonds, the molecule is entirely nonpolar and lacks any capacity for hydrogen bonding as either a donor or an acceptor. This medium-sized aliphatic structure is characterized by its uniform electron distribution and purely hydrophobic character.
